course_description.overview: Challenging non-competitive road sportive through the scenic Laurentian Mountains with distances from 45 km to 160 km Super Fondo, featuring smooth paved roads, significant climbs like Nordet sector, and exciting descents.

key_segments.name: Nordet Sector
key_segments.description: Significant uphill efforts followed by exciting descents on impeccable roads.

key_segments.name: Lac Quenouille ascent
key_segments.description: Previously featured but eliminated for better experience.

course_description.difficulty_narrative: Varied for all levels: Super Fondo (160 km) for experts, Gran Fondo (125 km) for experienced, Medio (80 km) intermediate-advanced, Molo (45 km) beginners; emphasizes safety, group riding, and personal challenge in beautiful landscapes.

logistics.getting_there: Accessible north of Montreal in Laurentians; fly into nearby airports and drive to Mont-Tremblant.
logistics.accommodation: Numerous hotels and resorts with participant discounts via Tourisme Mont-Tremblant; ideal for active weekends with family/friends.
logistics.registration_details: Opens early; secure spot before deadlines like July 3 for perks; max 2000 participants; visit granfondotremblant.ca.
logistics.bike_logistics: Devinci Sunset Ride recon on Friday; introductory clinics for peloton riding; bike-friendly resort setup.
logistics.local_considerations: Early season event with spring-like weather; festive weekend includes dinner, music, kiosks, Coffee Ride; partnerships with Desjardins, Devinci.

final_verdict.one_liner: Scenic, well-organized road sportive in Laurentians ideal for season-opening endurance challenge.
final_verdict.who_is_it_for: All levels of cyclists seeking safe group rides, stunning views, and personal tests from beginner to expert.
final_verdict.is_it_worth_it: Yes, for the beauty, organization, and community in a top destination resort.
pro_tips: Register early for popular slots and pelotons.
Book discounted lodging via Tourism Mont-Tremblant.
Join Friday Sunset Ride for recon and clinics.
Train for Nordet climbs and early-season endurance.
